match.userMessage: Show me all my Descript projects.

response.content: I'll use the listProjects tool to fetch all your Descript projects right now.

match.userMessage: Show details for project ID abc123.

response.content: I'll call getProject with ID abc123 to retrieve the full project details.

match.userMessage: Import this video into a new project called Demo Reel: https://example.com/video.mp4

response.content: I'll use importMedia to bring that video into a new project named Demo Reel.

match.userMessage: Edit project abc123 to remove all filler words using Underlord.

response.content: I'll run agentEdit on project abc123 with the prompt to remove all filler words via Underlord.

match.userMessage: Publish project abc123 as a 1080p video and give me the share link.

response.content: I'll call publish on project abc123 at 1080p Video resolution to generate a shareable link.

match.userMessage: What is the current status of job xyz789?

response.content: I'll use getJob to check the current status of job xyz789 for you.

match.userMessage: Show me the most recent jobs in my account.

response.content: I'll call listJobs to retrieve the most recent jobs from your account.
I'm sorry, but the Descript API does not support cancelling jobs. Once a job is running, it cannot be stopped via the API. You may be able to cancel it from the Descript Studio interface directly.
